configuration.secrets: inherited at runtime and excluded from evidence

three-node-approved-run.evidence: Copilot connected to the installed wheel, previewed and confirmed A/B-to-C, observed succeeded, and replayed the same run.

invalid-graphs.evidence: Copilot submitted the shared cycle case and received invalid_input/cycle with no run; the shared fixture gate also covered dangling_dependency.

status-and-list.evidence: Copilot inspected the canonical run and found its succeeded durable listing without raw provider payloads or credentials.

bounded-expansion.evidence: Copilot started the shared expansion fixture; A and CHILD succeeded with acceptedNodes=2, currentRound=2, maxConcurrent=1, and usedTokens=2. A second run proved maxNodes overflow appended no child.

interruption-recovery.evidence: Copilot started the run, the worker and sidecar were killed and restored, and Copilot then observed A/B before C with a durable succeeded state.

provider-substitution.evidence: Copilot ran the unchanged provider-neutral contract through alternate:deterministic and observed provider=alternate, model=deterministic, and normalized token usage.

safety.caps: node, round, concurrency, and token utilization remained within the shared limits
safety.redaction: authentication, signing, approval, and raw provider values were excluded

knownLimitations: The Copilot model/backend is external and may require up to three isolated retries; the native test is opt-in and can consume Copilot quota.
